focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ben offers to help Mrs. Coetzee with her son skipping school; Susan observes, then affectionately walks Ben to the car after discussing the situation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Churchgoer concerned about a community issue
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ben offers to visit Mrs. Coetzee's truant son to discuss his school absence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To visit and talk with Mrs. Coetzee's son about his school absence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben has arranged to phone Mrs. Coetzee to schedule a visit.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Mrs. Coetzee's son's refusal to attend school or church.
evidence_quotes: Ben is chatting, concerned, to a WOMAN.
He won't come to church. He lies in bed all day, listening to his headphones.
I wondered why he wasn't at school. Would it help if I came to see him? He's always seemed a good kid to me.
Of course. I'll phone and we can fix a time.

relation_updates: Ben agrees to help Mrs. Coetzee with her son's truancy.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gordon seeks justice for his son Jonathan's unjust caning, fearing a police record will harm his future. He emphasizes emotional pain over physical wounds. Ben reassures him, no record will be made. Johan assists with medicine.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Witness to Jonathan's caning injuries and advisor who dismisses legal action
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ben sees Jonathan's severe caning wounds, hears Gordon's claim of injustice, and rejects the idea of a lawyer while providing medical aid.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben has given iodine for Jonathan's wounds and assured Gordon there will be no police record, but refused to consider hiring a lawyer.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Gordon's concern about Jonathan's unjust punishment and potential police record, which Ben downplays as minor.
evidence_quotes: Ben is shocked by the severity of the canning.
BEN A lawyer? That won't heal Jonathan's buttocks.
BEN Don't worry, Gordon. I'm sure there'll be no record, it's such a minor case.

relation_updates: Gordon directly appeals to Ben for help with Jonathan's injustice.
Ben witnesses Jonathan's physical humiliation and pain.
status_updates: Ben becomes aware of a severe disciplinary injustice at his school.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A man shocked by the physical evidence but believing the disciplinary case is minor and won't have lasting.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gordon reveals Jonathan was arrested and has disappeared; Ben, unaware, expresses concern and decides to investigate, while Gordon remains silent and resigned.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Concerned employer
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ben learns from Gordon that Jonathan has been arrested and disappeared, and he decides to investigate.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To find out what happened to Jonathan.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben has committed to investigating Jonathan's disappearance.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jonathan's whereabouts and safety are unknown.
evidence_quotes: Ben does a double-take and stops playing.
He crosses the yard to Gordon
BEN Okay! I'll see what I can find out.

relation_updates: Gordon confides in Ben about Jonathan's disappearance, showing trust.
Ben's sense of responsibility for Jonathan deepens.
status_updates: Shifts from carefree play to active concern and determination.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A man who takes action when those connected to him are in trouble.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 28、EXT. DAN PIENAAR SCHOOL - PLAYING FIELDS - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gordon demands to know how his son died during the riots, defying official accounts, and leaves on a mower to seek the truth, while Ben and the headmaster express concern over the volatile situation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Ben is confronted by Gordon's demand for truth about his son's death.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ben offers comfort to Gordon, but Gordon rejects it and insists on knowing how his son died, leaving Ben helpless and warned by Cloete to be careful.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben watches Gordon drive away on a mower to seek the truth alone.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Gordon's quest for truth about his son's death.
evidence_quotes: What can we do, Gordon? You or I... We can't change it.
How did he die, Mister Ben, sir?
leaving Ben -- helpless -- behind him -- watching.
This business of Gordon's son. Be careful, Ben. These are not normal times -- one has to make allowances.

relation_updates: Gordon rejects Ben's comfort and demands truth.
Cloete warns Ben to be careful.
status_updates: Ben feels helpless in the face of injustice.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Ben is unable to provide answers or help within the system, as shown by his helplessness and Cloete's.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ben fills out a slip under police surveillance, observes a detained African girl taken to the 10th floor, receives stamped approval, and is directed to the second floor where someone will meet him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Ben visits John Vorster Square police headquarters to seek information.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ben fills out a visitor slip under police surveillance, notices a video camera, observes a detained African girl being taken to the 10th floor, and receives stamped approval to proceed.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To get information about Gordon from the police.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben is inside the police building, directed to the second floor for a meeting.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: What will Ben learn from the police?
evidence_quotes: Ben fills in a slip
Ben notices a video surveillance camera
Ben watches them enter the lift and follows the progress of lift to the 10th floor
Ben enters a lift

relation_updates: Interacts with police bureaucracy.
status_updates: Official visitor to police headquarters.
Witnesses detention of African girl.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A man who directly engages with police bureaucracy to seek answers.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stanley informs Ben and Susan of Gordon's alleged suicide; Emily was not notified by authorities, learning via radio, prompting Ben's silent distress and Stanley's follow-up actions.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Ben learns from Stanley that Gordon has died in custody.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley informs Ben that Gordon allegedly committed suicide, shocking Ben and revealing authorities failed to notify Gordon's family.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben is left speechless and walks out in deep thought after hearing the news.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The suspicious circumstances of Gordon's death and the authorities' failure to inform his family.
evidence_quotes: STANLEY Gordon's dead.
STANLEY The bastards say he committed suicide... hanged himself.
STANLEY They didn't tell her. She heard it on the radio like the rest of us.
Ben, recovering from the shock. BEN Suicide... is that what they told Emily -- poor woman...

relation_updates: Marital strain with Susan over secrecy and obsession.
Deepened bond with Stanley through shared tragedy.
status_updates: Gordon is dead.
Ben's protective efforts have failed.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A man shocked by the death of someone he tried to protect and disturbed by how the news.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ben, horrified, confirms Gordon's identity after the undertaker reveals the body's injuries; Stanley directs the examination, and they leave after Ben accepts the grim reality.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Observer of Gordon's corpse at the funeral parlor
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ben views Gordon's battered body, reacts with horror, and now believes Gordon was murdered.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben leaves the funeral parlor after confirming Gordon's identity and the injuries on his body.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to act on the evidence of Gordon's murder.
evidence_quotes: Ben who looks at the wounds with horror
Ben's ashen.
He shuts his eyes tightly.
He must accept that this battered corpse is Gordon.

status_updates: Now believes Gordon was murdered.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A man confronted with undeniable evidence of Gordon's violent death.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The magistrate rules no conclusive evidence links the Security Police to Gordon Ngubene's death, attributing it to suicide by hanging based on medical evidence, and clears all parties of criminal conduct.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Observer during magistrate's verdict in courtroom
timeline_nodes.salient_development: The magistrate rules Gordon Ngubene's death a suicide and clears Security Police of wrongdoing.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The court has exonerated the Security Police, closing the legal case.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Ben will respond to the legal defeat.
evidence_quotes: he concludes that Gordon Ngubene committed suicide and that no criminal act or omission by any individual can be substantiated.
Ben's tense expression

auxiliary.persona_anchor: A man tense and observant during an unfavorable verdict.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Susan and Ben argue over his dedication to his work and racial politics; she accuses him of neglecting their family, he leaves for Soweto, deepening their rift.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Ben's insistence on pursuing justice for Gordon and Jonathan leads to a heated argument with Susan, resulting in him leaving for Soweto.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After Susan tells him to go live in Soweto, Ben strides out of the room, stating he is going to Soweto.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To continue seeking justice for Gordon and Jonathan despite family conflict.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben has left the bedroom for Soweto, and his relationship with Susan is severely strained.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The conflict between Ben's dedication to racial justice and his family obligations, as shown in the argument.
evidence_quotes: BEN (without looking) Soweto!

relation_updates: Relationship with Susan becomes openly adversarial.
Ben physically leaves family home for Soweto.
status_updates: Ben's marital relationship fractures irreparably.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A man who prioritizes his principles over domestic harmony.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ben defies Stolz's threats, reaffirming his commitment to uncovering the truth despite risks to his safety; Stolz leaves a card with his private contact, warning of consequences, but Ben remains resolute.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Investigator resisting blackmail to continue his inquiry
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ben rejects Stolz's blackmail attempt with a compromising photograph and asserts his determination to uncover the truth about Gordon Ngubene's case.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To uncover the truth about Gordon Ngubene's case without yielding to threats.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben remains resolute in his investigation after Stolz leaves, with the photograph still in Stolz's possession.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The compromising photograph is retained by Stolz, leaving a personal threat unresolved.

relation_updates: Adversarial relationship with Stolz becomes explicit and confrontational.
status_updates: Personal vulnerability exposed via compromising photograph, but Ben refuses to yield.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A defiant individual who refuses to be blackmailed, even when his personal life is exposed.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Johan retrieves a hidden envelope with crucial papers, reuniting with Ben who thanks him emotionally as the crowd disperses.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father returning to a bombed home, supported by his son who saved crucial papers.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ben's home and study are bombed, but Johan has hidden and retrieved the crucial investigation papers, leading to an emotional embrace and Ben's gratitude.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben is emotionally overwhelmed but has the evidence papers in hand and has shared a moment of thanks with Johan.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The hostile crowd and the bombing indicate ongoing danger and community opposition for Ben.
evidence_quotes: Ben and Johan arrive at the house to find an unsympathetic small crowd waiting, murmuring in Afrikaans and watching with hostility.
Ben sinks onto a stool in total defeat, overwhelmed by the destruction and the silence that follows.
He approaches Ben and hands it over—inside is the file containing all the crucial papers.
BEN, voice trembling with emotion, tells Johan, 'Thank you, son. You did a man's job.'

relation_updates: Father-son bond strengthened as Johan proves his capability and loyalty.
Hostile community relationship evident from the crowd.
status_updates: Home and study destroyed, but crucial evidence preserved by Johan.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A father who, despite being defeated by destruction, finds strength in his son's action and loyalty.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Captain Stolz drives into cafe street, sees Ben crossing, accelerates, hits him, and flees; bystanders gather as Ben lies injured.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Victim of a deliberate vehicular assault by Captain Stolz
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ben is struck by Captain Stolz's car, thrown into the air, and left injured as bystanders gather.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben is lying injured on the street with a crowd gathering around him.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ben's injury and Captain Stolz's escape from the scene.
evidence_quotes: Ben is hurrying across the road to his car.
Captain Stolz sees him, accelerates and hits Ben, hurling him high into the air.
People rush to Ben's side...

relation_updates: Conflict with Captain Stolz escalates to violent physical assault.
status_updates: Ben is injured and incapacitated on the street.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Ben Du Toit evolves from a well-meaning Afrikaans teacher who intervenes in community problems to a determined investigator seeking justice for his gardener Gordon Ngubene and Gordon's son Jonathan. His journey involves confronting police brutality, systemic injustice, and personal risk, leading to his social ostracization, family breakdown, and eventual targeting by the state. He ultimately pays with his life after being deliberately run over by Captain Stolz.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 1、EXT. DAN PIENAAR SECONDARY SCHOOL FOR BOYS - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: At a Johannesburg school, Jonathan is tripped by a student, reacts aggressively, and walks off despite his father's shout. The headmaster observes but takes no action.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: School laborer and father
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gordon witnesses Jonathan being tripped by a student, reacting aggressively, and walking off in defiance, while showing deference to employer Ben Du Toit.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jonathan walks off school grounds in anger, ignoring Gordon's shout.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jonathan's defiance and the headmaster's inaction after the incident.
evidence_quotes: Ben stops to talk to Gordon who jumps to his feet.
GORDON (straightening himself) He's working hard, Mr. Ben'sir, and your money will not be wasted. Emily and me will always thank you.
Gordon shouts "Jonathan."
Jonathan turns to look at his father and continues to walk off.

relation_updates: Established deferential relationship with employer Ben Du Toit
Father-son dynamic strained by Jonathan's public defiance
status_updates: Working as gardener at Dan Pienaar school
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Deferential laborer and protective father

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gordon seeks justice for his son Jonathan's unjust caning, fearing a police record will harm his future. He emphasizes emotional pain over physical wounds. Ben reassures him, no record will be made. Johan assists with medicine.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father showing son's caning wounds and advocating for justice
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gordon reveals Jonathan's severe caning wounds to Ben, insists the punishment was unjust, and expresses fear that a police record will harm Jonathan's future job prospects.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To get Ben's help in ensuring no police record is created and to have the injustice acknowledged.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben provides iodine for the wounds and reassures Gordon that no police record will be made, but the injustice remains unaddressed.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The unjust caning and the fear of a permanent police record affecting Jonathan's future.
evidence_quotes: He says he wasn't doing anything wrong, Mister Ben, sir. And I believe him, I know my son! It's an injustice!
Don't worry, Gordon. I'm sure there'll be no record, it's such a minor case. Please don't worry.

relation_updates: Confides in Ben about racial injustice and seeks his intervention
Trusts Ben enough to show son's wounds and express deep fears
status_updates: In Ben's kitchen discussing personal family crisis
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Father advocating for son's redress

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: In a Soweto house, Gordon asks a girl if Jonathan and Wellington were taken; she confirms, glancing at her father who encourages her to speak.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father verifying son's detention with a witness
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gordon asks a girl if Jonathan was taken, and she confirms it was Jonathan with Wellington.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To confirm the identity and circumstances of his son's detention
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gordon receives confirmation that Jonathan was taken by police along with Wellington.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The fate of Jonathan and how to secure his release
evidence_quotes: GORDON (to girl) Are you sure it was Jonathan they took away?
GIRL (DAUGHTER) Yes, baba, with Wellington.

relation_updates: Father-son relationship strained by detention
status_updates: Father of detained son
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Anxious father seeking direct confirmation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A woman collapses in grief while identifying her child's body; comforted by another, she's pulled away as the assistant tags the body. Gordon checks the last drawer—Jonathan is not among the dead—and they exit past mourning parents.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father searching for missing son in morgue
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gordon checks the last drawer in the morgue and confirms Jonathan is not among the dead.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gordon and Emily exit the morgue, with Jonathan's status as not dead confirmed, but his location still unknown.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Where is Jonathan
evidence_quotes: Gordon looks into the last drawer, Jonathan is not there.
They make their way out past the other parents and a group of mourning women sitting.

relation_updates: Reinforces co-parent relationship with Emily
Confirms professional relationship with lawyer Julius
status_updates: Emotional state: grief and anger
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Father experiencing grief and anger while searching for his missing son.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gordon reveals Jonathan was arrested and has disappeared; Ben, unaware, expresses concern and decides to investigate, while Gordon remains silent and resigned.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father informing Ben about son's arrest and disappearance
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gordon tells Ben that Jonathan was arrested by police and has disappeared, leading Ben to decide to investigate.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To have Ben help find Jonathan
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gordon has disclosed Jonathan's disappearance to Ben, and Ben has agreed to look into it.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jonathan's whereabouts and condition are unknown
evidence_quotes: GORDON I don't know, Mister Ben, sir -- the police took him.
GORDON They arrested many. They even deny they've got him. He's disappeared...

relation_updates: Gordon trusts Ben with critical family information
Ben becomes actively involved in search
status_updates: Son officially missing after police arrest
Gordon now dependent on Ben's intervention
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Father relying on Ben's intervention after son's arrest

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Emily senses something is wrong when Gordon approaches; she breaks down, and he leads her to the house as Margaret helps comfort her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Husband whose return home causes his wife to break down
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gordon approaches the house, Emily breaks down upon seeing him, he embraces her, and with Margaret's help, leads her inside
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gordon is leading Emily into their home, assisted by neighbor Margaret
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The specific reason for Emily's distress and Gordon's slow approach is not stated
evidence_quotes: Gordon sees her and stops.
Gordon hurries to embrace her.
Gordon starts to lead her to the house.

relation_updates: Gordon and Emily share a moment of shared grief and mutual support as he embraces her
status_updates: Gordon's demeanor signals a major tragedy to his family
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Comforting husband

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Emily sits motionless in anger as cars drive away; her neighbor Margaret appears at the door after police arrest Gordon and the cleaner.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Arrested suspect
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gordon is confronted by Captain Stolz and police who enter his home and order him to stand, indicating his arrest.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Being taken into custody by police.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: What will happen to him during detention.
evidence_quotes: Gordon is reading aloud from a handwritten document.
STOLZ (to Gordon) On your feet! So, you must be Gordon Ngubene?

relation_updates: Directly confronted by Capt. Stolz
Taken into custody by African Security Police
status_updates: Handcuffed by police
Physically removed from home
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A suspect facing re-arrest after recounting past torture.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gordon is tortured with electric shocks by Stolz and Venter during interrogation about activists; a letter arrives via Seroke, but no reply is given. Gordon denies A.N.C. involvement and screams under shock.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Detainee subjected to electric shock interrogation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gordon is tortured with electric shocks by Captain Stolz and Lieutenant Venter, who demand names of activists.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To resist revealing information about activists during interrogation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: In severe pain from electric shocks but denies A.N.C. involvement.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether continued torture will force him to disclose information.
evidence_quotes: Gordon is given a short burst of electric shock.
GORDON I don't know anything about the A.N.C.
Gordon suddenly shakes violently and shouts repeatedly. GORDON Hai! Hai! Hai!

relation_updates: Tortured by Captain Stolz and Lieutenant Venter
status_updates: Subjected to electric shock torture
Physically restrained and assaulted
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Tortured detainee resisting coercion.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stanley informs Ben and Susan of Gordon's alleged suicide; Emily was not notified by authorities, learning via radio, prompting Ben's silent distress and Stanley's follow-up actions.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Deceased detainee reported to have committed suicide
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gordon's death in custody is announced as suicide by hanging
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Deceased
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Lack of official notification to family and questions about the true circumstances of death
evidence_quotes: Gordon's dead.
The bastards say he committed suicide... hanged himself.
They didn't tell her. She heard it on the radio like the rest of us.

relation_updates: Emily becomes his widow
status_updates: Dead in custody
Official cause: suicide by hanging
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Deceased individual in police custody

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ben, horrified, confirms Gordon's identity after the undertaker reveals the body's injuries; Stanley directs the examination, and they leave after Ben accepts the grim reality.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Deceased with visible injuries from beating
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ben sees Gordon's bruised and battered chest, along with other injuries, and is forced to accept that the corpse is Gordon despite its horrific condition.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben acknowledges Gordon's body as battered and confirms his death.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The violent evidence on Gordon's body versus any prior assumptions about his death.
evidence_quotes: The man opens Gordon's shirt and reveals the bruised and battered chest.
Ben who looks at the wounds with horror
the undertaker opens the shirt to the waist.

status_updates: Body shows extensive injuries: bruised chest, skull stitches, facial scarring
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Victim of violent death

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 79、INT. COURT ROOM - FIRST DAY OF INQUEST - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stolz requests to leave to escort detainee Archibald back to John Vorster Square; the magistrate adjourns the hearing until 2:30, met with crowd laughter.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Deceased subject of inquest testimony
timeline_nodes.salient_development: At inquest, police captain testifies Gordon Ngubene resisted arrest violently and committed suicide in cell, supported by a presented suicide note; police physician testifies only to treating toothache.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Inquest adjourned; police narrative of suicide entered into record.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Discrepancy between police account of Gordon's violence and the superficial medical examination.
evidence_quotes: He then became violent and resisted arrest.
The next morning we found him dead in his cell.
'Dear Captain. I prefer to die rather than betray any more of my friends. Amandla! Gordon Ngubene.'
I extracted three decayed teeth, and gave him aspirin for the pain... that's all.

relation_updates: Officially portrayed as ANC-linked terrorist
status_updates: Official cause: suicide in cell
Suicide note presented as evidence
Police testify he resisted arrest violently
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Subject of contested suicide narrative

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Gordon begins as a school laborer and father, advocating for his son Jonathan after unjust punishment. Following Jonathan's detention, disappearance, and death in custody, Gordon seeks truth and is arrested, tortured, and dies under suspicious circumstances. His death is officially ruled suicide, but evidence of violence persists, challenging the official narrative.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stanley comforts a woman, Paulina, whose 13-year-old daughter was taken, as they both seek help at the police station where officers provide little assistance.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Stanley at the police station, seeking information on missing persons and offering support to others.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley questions Sergeant Van Zyl about missing persons lists and other locations like John Voster Square, then comforts Paulina who is searching for her 13-year-old daughter.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Find missing persons through police inquiries.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Police provide no useful information; Stanley shifts to comforting Paulina, another affected community member.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Missing persons not located through police channels.
evidence_quotes: STANLEY No, no, I understand, Baas, but is that all the names? There's no other list somewhere?
SERGEANT VAN ZYL I'm telling you. He's not in custody. Have you tried the hospital? Have you tried the mortuary?
STANLEY You're here too, sis Paulina, who are you looking for?
STANLEY (comforting her) We are all searching.

relation_updates: Established familial bond with Paulina (addressed as 'sis')
status_updates: Actively engaged in missing persons search
Positioned as community supporter at police station
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Persistent and comforting community advocate.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stanley informs Gordon and Wellington that Jonathan is dead; Gordon anxiously seeks details about his death, while Wellington expresses surprise, having last seen him weeks ago.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Informs Gordon and Wellington of Jonathan's death and observes Wellington's injury
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley tells Gordon and Wellington that Jonathan is dead and notices Wellington's deep facial scar
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Has confirmed Jonathan's death and seen physical evidence of torture on Wellington
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Jonathan died and the cause of Wellington's injuries
evidence_quotes: Stanley notices a deep scar from the forehead to the cheek.
STANLEY What happened... Don't tell me...
STANLEY Jonathan is dead.

relation_updates: Confirms Jonathan's death to Gordon
Witnesses Wellington's injuries
status_updates: Knows Jonathan is dead
Has seen physical evidence of torture

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stanley informs Ben and Susan of Gordon's alleged suicide; Emily was not notified by authorities, learning via radio, prompting Ben's silent distress and Stanley's follow-up actions.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Messenger informing Ben of Gordon's death.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley tells Ben that Gordon is dead, allegedly by suicide, and that Emily was not notified by authorities, prompting Ben's shocked response.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben walks out of the garage in deep thought, with Stanley following.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The circumstances of Gordon's death and the failure to officially notify Emily.
evidence_quotes: STANLEY Gordon's dead.
STANLEY The bastards say he committed suicide... hanged himself.
Ben walks slowly out of the garage in deep thoughts -- Stanley follows him.

relation_updates: Strengthens trust with Ben through shared concern
Demonstrates loyalty to Gordon and Emily
status_updates: Gordon is dead
Stanley acts as key informant

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ben seeks to see Gordon despite Stanley's warnings of danger; Stanley reveals familial bonds among the oppressed, hinting at collective resistance and a final farewell amid Soweto unrest.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Brother of Gordon and confrontational advisor to Ben
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley reveals he is Gordon's brother and states the community shares this bond, while warning Ben of riots in Soweto.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To handle Gordon's death collectively in the African way, as Stanley asserts.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben has received Stanley's warning about Soweto dangers, and Stanley has asserted his role in the collective handling of Gordon's affairs.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ben's insistence on going to Soweto and the collective response to Gordon's death.
evidence_quotes: I'm his brother, man, we all are! We'll take care of everything. (with pride) That's the African way.
Don't look for trouble, man. You know there are riots all over Soweto.

relation_updates: Discloses kinship with Gordon
Relationship with Ben becomes strained and adversarial
status_updates: Stanley is Gordon's brother
Involved in collective resistance
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Confrontational and proud, emphasizing African communal values.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ben, horrified, confirms Gordon's identity after the undertaker reveals the body's injuries; Stanley directs the examination, and they leave after Ben accepts the grim reality.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Director of the body viewing and intermediary with the undertaker
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley commands the undertaker to reveal Gordon's injuries, observes Ben's horrified reaction, and leads their departure after Ben accepts the reality.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To ensure Ben views and acknowledges Gordon's battered corpse.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben has confronted Gordon's body and accepted his death; they leave the funeral parlor together.
evidence_quotes: Stanley speaks in an African language to the undertaker.
Stanley observes Ben who looks at the wounds with horror.
Then another command from Stanley
Stanley thanks the man and turns to leave.

relation_updates: Stanley acts as an intermediary between Ben and the undertaker
Stanley's relationship with Ben becomes strained and adversarial as he denies Ben's request
status_updates: Stanley directs the examination of Gordon's body
Stanley assumes a protective, controlling role over Ben's actions in Soweto

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ben returns home distraught, claiming Gordon was murdered and his people lied, mentions Dr. Hassiem's involvement, while Susan pleads with him to stop investigating, fearing for their safety, and Johan tries to comfort Ben with a drink.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Co-conspirator and protector
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley explains that Dr. Hassiem represented Emily at Gordon's autopsy, warns Ben that authorities may retaliate, and gives him a cigarette packet with covert contact instructions.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To warn Ben of danger and set up a secret way to communicate.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Stanley drives away, leaving Ben with the contact method and a plan to meet at ten the next day.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The threat to Ben's safety and the need to access Dr. Hassiem's report.
evidence_quotes: Dr. Hassiem is the doctor we got to represent Emily at Gordon's autopsy.
Now, be careful. They will put their marks on you!
In case you need me. Don't give your name -- just say 'Lanie' phoned -- right?
Stanley drives vigorously away.

relation_updates: Stanley and Ben establish a covert communication plan
Stanley warns Ben of surveillance and retaliation
status_updates: Stanley becomes more deeply involved in the investigation
Stanley's cynicism about the system is expressed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Stanley shows cynicism about the legal system but acts to protect Ben.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stanley mocks the Special Branch's incompetence, comforts Ben about their search, and welcomes him with a handshake after Ben admits he wasn't scared.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Resistance comrade and mentor to Ben
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley mocks the Special Branch's incompetence and, after Ben admits he wasn't scared by their search, offers a handshake to welcome him into the resistance.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ben is welcomed into the resistance 'club' through a handshake with Stanley.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ongoing surveillance and intimidation by Special Branch, as indicated by the search of Ben's house.
evidence_quotes: STANLEY Hallelujah! (offers his huge hand) Shake, man. Join the club.

relation_updates: Deepens bond with Ben through shared resistance stance
Offers Ben membership in resistance 'club'
status_updates: Positioned as experienced resistance figure
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Experienced resistance figure who uses humor to build solidarity and recruit.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stanley calmly endures a hostile police search, retrieves his discarded passbook after officers leave, and reflects quietly as they drive away.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Target of police harassment
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley complies with police orders to search his car and inspect his passbook, responding with humor and calm under direct threat.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Police drive away after finding nothing; Stanley retrieves his discarded passbook.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Police warning implies ongoing surveillance and threat.
evidence_quotes: Good evening. I'd like to invite you into my humble home, but it's full of terrorists.
Stanley executes the order.
Stanley doesn't pick it up and just watches the man.
Stanley looks at them thoughtfully, then picks up his book.

relation_updates: Hostile confrontation with Jaimie and police
status_updates: Subjected to aggressive police search of car and person
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Uses humor and maintains composure during hostile confrontation.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stanley reveals Emily's death from a broken heart, leaving Ben devastated. Ben's in-laws and Susan leave, while Johan watches silently as grief unfolds.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Stanley informs Ben of Emily's death
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley tells Ben that Emily is dead, changing from laughter to crying
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Stanley is sobbing after revealing Emily's death to Ben
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Emily's death is attributed to 'a broken heart,' but no further details are given
evidence_quotes: STANLEY (sitting beside Ben) You joking! (he laughs) Shit! I fucked it all up for you. Sorry, man. He laughs again.
STANLEY Emily is dead.
Stanley doesn't answer - he cries.
STANLEY (between two sobs) A broken heart. All they said.

relation_updates: Stanley's relationship with Ben deepens through shared grief and confrontation
status_updates: Stanley transitions from drunken mockery to emotional vulnerability

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stanley and Ben discuss Johnson Seroke's murder, linking it to Stolz; multiple allies detained or dead, they vow to continue clearing Gordon's name despite escalating risks.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Investigator sharing news of an ally's murder and urging continued investigation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stanley shows Ben a newspaper report of Johnson Seroke's murder, details other allies detained or missing, and insists they persist in clearing Gordon's name.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To continue investigating despite the deaths and detentions of allies.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Stanley and Ben reaffirm their commitment to the investigation after discussing the escalating threats.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Who among their remaining allies might be targeted next.
evidence_quotes: STANLEY Late at night. A knock on the door. He opened and five shots, point-blank range. Face, chest, stomach.

relation_updates: Stanley and Ben reaffirm their partnership in the face of growing threats
status_updates: Stanley becomes the bearer of bad news about their allies' fates

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Stanley, a Soweto taxi driver and community advocate, begins by navigating police bureaucracy to find missing persons, then becomes a key figure in investigating the death of his brother Gordon. He develops a complex, often adversarial relationship with white lawyer Ben, guiding him through Soweto's realities while enduring police harassment. Stanley's journey culminates in deep involvement in the resistance, personal losses, and a final commitment to continue the fight despite escalating dangers.
